renderConfig.buildCommand: npm ci --include=dev && npm run verify:typecheck && npm test -- --runInBand tests/scale/bffStagingServerBoundary.test.ts tests/scale/bffStagingHttpServer.test.ts
renderConfig.instanceTypeReason: Smallest always-on paid instance is recommended for staging smoke to avoid cold-start/sleep false negatives.
renderConfig.autoDeployReason: Disable for first staging smoke; enable later only after /health, /ready, and shadow parity are green.

copyPasteReady.Build Command: npm ci --include=dev && npm run verify:typecheck && npm test -- --runInBand tests/scale/bffStagingServerBoundary.test.ts tests/scale/bffStagingHttpServer.test.ts

manualSetupChecklist: Root Directory: leave blank; package.json and scripts/server are at repo root.
Build Command: npm ci --include=dev && npm run verify:typecheck && npm test -- --runInBand tests/scale/bffStagingServerBoundary.test.ts tests/scale/bffStagingHttpServer.test.ts.
After deploy, manually check /ready before any shadow parity.
Instance Type: Starter for first smoke.
Auto Deploy: No for the first smoke.
Add only env names listed in renderEnvVarNames; do not paste env values into artifacts or chat.
Do not set or invent STAGING_BFF_BASE_URL until Render creates the service URL.
Keep BFF_MUTATION_ENABLED, BFF_IDEMPOTENCY_METADATA_ENABLED, and BFF_RATE_LIMIT_METADATA_ENABLED in disabled posture for smoke.
Do not route mobile traffic to BFF after deploy; a later health/readiness wave must prove the live URL first.
Do not enable Redis, Queue, idempotency persistence, rate enforcement, or external observability providers in this deploy config wave.
